invariant: A cycle may repeat only with new evidence, a materially different hypothesis, or measurable improvement.

banks.candidate: Redacted bounded signals awaiting adjudication; presence is not defect proof.
banks.classifierDispute: False positives, false negatives, exceptions, and insufficient-context cases.
banks.golden: Adjudicated immutable fixtures with stable expected properties and provenance.
banks.regression: Previously proven behavior and safety properties that every proposed improvement must preserve.
banks.insight: Cross-harness processing differences and reusable strategies awaiting bounded implementation.

comparabilityRequires: identical model id and model artifact/version
identical starting state and route contract
equivalent tool capability schema and authority
identical token, tool-call, wall-clock, and retry budgets
recorded prompts, tool calls, evidence, costs, and terminal outcomes
benchmarkMatrix.rankingRule: No harness is ranked or mined for insights unless every comparability requirement is proven.
benchmarkMatrix.extractionRule: Transfer generic strategies and contracts into V5, never product-specific strings or unproved outputs.

diversityRequires: one shared benchmark insight and generic V5 contract target
a distinct declared strategy hypothesis per variant
independent isolated workspace and candidate digest per variant
identical immutable tuning and holdout fixtures
identical model artifact, tools, authority, and resource budgets
no cross-variant state, patch, prompt, or result leakage before completion
improvementMatrix.regressionRule: Every variant returns through Loop 4 and receives its own digest-bound regression proof; one proof cannot authorize another variant.
improvementMatrix.promotionRule: Rank only regression-passing variants and promote the smallest generic improvement with the best grounded multi-objective result.

loops.purpose: Produce user value while capturing weak machine and human signals as fixture candidates.
rules: Human affect is a low-confidence candidate signal, never defect proof.
A machine warning must cite a violated contract or remain an unclassified signal.
Candidate capture must not replace delivery of the best bounded honest answer.

loops.purpose: Determine whether a candidate represents an agent defect, harness defect, justified exception, or insufficient evidence.
rules: Judge the action using evidence available at action time, not hindsight.
Run deterministic contract checks before model judgment.
The warning-producing classifier cannot independently confirm itself.
A raw candidate cannot enter the golden bank without adjudication.

loops.purpose: Run nine comparable harnesses in isolated parallel workspaces against the same Frontier GLM-5.2 model and immutable fixtures, then explain processing differences.
rules: All nine harnesses must use the declared identical model and benchmark contract.
Each harness runs in its own disposable safe-lab workspace.
Unavailable or non-comparable harnesses are reported, never silently substituted.
Compare processing trajectories, evidence use, recovery, cost, and outcome rather than prose style alone.
Extract generic strategies into the insight bank without changing V5 in this loop.

loops.purpose: Prove that proposed V5 improvements preserve all prior grounded, safety, cost, and convergence gains before promotion can be considered.
rules: The regression bank is immutable for the duration of a comparison.
Baseline and candidate use identical fixtures, model, budgets, tools, and starting state.
No safety, proof, or grounded-completion regression is allowed.
Performance tradeoffs require an explicit predeclared tolerance; hidden averaging cannot mask a severe regression.
Loop 5 cannot promote without a passing Loop 4 gate bound to the exact candidate digest.

loops.purpose: Generate nine isolated parallel implementations of the same generic benchmark insight for V5, regress each candidate independently, and promote only the strongest non-regressing improvement.
rules: Every variant cites the benchmark insight and generic contract it implements.
Exactly nine variants use the same GLM-5.2 model, fixtures, authority, and budgets in isolated parallel workspaces.
Each variant declares a materially distinct strategy hypothesis before observing another variant's result.
Product-specific strings and fixture-specific overfitting are forbidden.
Fewer warnings alone is not an improvement.
A separate passing Loop 4 proof for each exact candidate digest is mandatory before ranking.
A regression failure disqualifies only its bound variant and cannot be averaged away.
Only a reviewed promotion may update V5 defaults or canonical fixture classifiers.

crossLoopTransitions.condition: all candidate digests have typed Loop 4 outcomes
